instruction: It was warm yesterday but it's going to freeze tonight. What should I do with my irrigation system?
response: Great question! If you've already turned on your irrigation system and a freeze is coming, you should shut off the water supply to your system right away. Go to the location inside your home where the main water supply valve for your irrigation system is located - typically in the basement, laundry room, or possibly a closet. Turn that valve off by making sure it's at a ninety-degree angle to the pipe. This can help you avoid costly irrigation repairs!

instruction: How do I temporarily shut down my sprinkler system to avoid freeze damage?
response: It's actually pretty straightforward! Go to the location inside your home where the main water supply valve for your irrigation system is located. This is typically in the basement, laundry room, or possibly a closet. Turn that valve off by making sure it is at a ninety-degree angle to the pipe. This simple step can save you from costly irrigation repairs when Colorado's unpredictable weather brings a late freeze.

instruction: Where is the main water supply valve for my irrigation system usually located?
response: Typically, the main water supply valve for your irrigation system is located inside your home - often in the basement, laundry room, or possibly a closet. When you need to shut it off, turn the valve so it's at a ninety-degree angle to the pipe. If you need help locating it or have other irrigation questions, give us a call at (303) 993-8717!

instruction: Can a late spring freeze damage my sprinkler system?
response: It definitely can! Colorado weather is unpredictable - it can be 70 degrees one day and below freezing the next. If you've already fired up your irrigation system for early spring watering and a freeze is coming, you should temporarily shut off the water supply to your system to avoid costly repairs. We're here to help if you need assistance! Call Denver Sprinkler and Landscape at (303) 993-8717.

instruction: Why should I shut off my irrigation system before a freeze?
response: Shutting off your irrigation system before a freeze is one of the best ways to avoid costly irrigation repairs. When water freezes in your lines and valves, it can cause cracking and serious damage. Colorado springs are famous for sudden temperature drops, so it's important to be prepared. If you need help with your system, give us a call at (303) 993-8717 and we'll take care of it for you.

instruction: How should I be watering my lawn and landscape in Denver?
response: The key rule of thumb is simple: water infrequently and heavy! If you can follow that simple philosophy, you'll be successful in keeping your lawn and landscape healthy. Don't overcomplicate it. Watering deeply but less frequently encourages stronger root growth and a healthier landscape overall. If you need help setting up your irrigation system to do this automatically, give us a call at (303) 993-8717!

instruction: What's the best watering strategy for my Denver lawn?
response: It's actually not complicated at all! The best approach is to water "infrequently and heavy." That's the golden rule of thumb for Denver homeowners. If you can stick to that philosophy, you'll be successful in keeping your lawn and landscape looking great. Deep, less frequent watering promotes healthier root systems and stronger turf.

instruction: Is there a way to automate my lawn watering so I don't have to use garden hoses?
response: There sure is! An automated irrigation system is the answer - it waters your lawn on a schedule so you don't have to spend your days pulling garden hoses around. The system gets buried in the ground so it stays out of sight when not in use. Denver Sprinkler and Landscape can install and maintain an automated irrigation system for you. Call us at (303) 993-8717 to learn more!

instruction: Am I overwatering my lawn? How much should I water?
response: A lot of Denver homeowners worry about this, but it's actually simple. The rule of thumb is to water "infrequently and heavy." Many people make the mistake of watering too often with light sprinkles, which doesn't encourage deep root growth. Instead, give your lawn a good, deep watering less frequently. If you need help dialing in your irrigation schedule, give us a call at (303) 993-8717!

instruction: Do you have tips for watering my landscape plants versus my lawn?
response: Great question! The same basic principle applies to both your lawn and your landscape: water infrequently and heavy. This encourages deeper root growth for both turf and plants. If you can follow that simple rule of thumb, you'll be successful with both your lawn and landscape.
